audit_findings.finding: _strike_count non-atomic write can lose strike count under concurrent Stop hooks, defeating MAX_STRIKES deadlock prevention (same pattern as case-404 pre-fix in discovery-gate)
audit_findings.remediation: Use tempfile.mkstemp + os.fdopen + os.replace atomic write pattern consistent with save-checkpoint._atomic_write_json

audit_findings.finding: py_compile invoked without -- terminator; filenames starting with - misinterpreted as CLI options causing false-positive syntax errors triggering Stop gate blocks (case-472 fixed same bug in stop-completion-gate but not synced here)

_GIT_INVOKED regex and _parse_git_cwd are oversimplified vs autonomous-commit-gate: no /usr/bin/git full-path recognition, no command-chain segment evaluation, no eval/sh-c unwrapping, no $() substitution extraction — allows bypass of studio pipeline stage constraints
audit_findings.remediation: Extract shared _git_cmd_parser.py from autonomous-commit-gate or minimally add basename-git recognition + shell-op segment splitting

audit_findings.finding: from pathlib import Path inside if __name__=='__main__' guard; find_task_files references Path at line 140 — works today because hook runs as __main__ but fragile under refactor
audit_findings.remediation: Move Path import to module-level imports section

audit_findings.finding: Audit log template code duplicated across 6 hook files (autonomous-commit-gate, discovery-gate, pipeline-gate, auto-commit, notify-phone, codegraph-sync) — DRY violation, schema drift risk
audit_findings.remediation: Extract shared _audit_log_helper.py with emit_audit() function (structural debt SD-003)
